expectations.text: Produces both deliverables — a markdown behavior spec (.md) and a Cucumber .feature file
expectations.evidence: Output dir contains password-reset-behavior-spec.md and password-reset.feature.

expectations.text: The .feature file is well-formed Gherkin (Feature declaration, Scenario blocks, Given/When/Then steps)
expectations.evidence: password-reset.feature opens with 'Feature:' + 'Background:' and contains 43 well-formed Scenario/Scenario Outline blocks with Given/When/Then/And steps and an Examples table.

expectations.text: Happy-path scenarios are tagged @happy-path and match the forgot-password flow described in the prompt
expectations.evidence: 3 @happy-path scenarios: request reset link, choose new password from valid link, complete reset and land on dashboard — matches the prompt's flow.

expectations.text: The spec includes a coverage checklist enumerating all 14 deviation taxonomy categories
expectations.evidence: Section 9 Coverage Checklist has all 14 numbered category rows, each marked Yes with a scenario count.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios span at least 8 distinct deviation categories
Deviation scenario Then-steps specify concrete graceful product behavior, not merely that an error occurred
expectations.evidence: e.g. malformed-email scenario keeps the typed value 'in the field for correction'; email-provider-down scenario queues the email 'for automatic retry' and shows the standard confirmation — resolutions, not just error reports.

expectations.text: Genuine product decisions are tagged @assumption and consolidated into a Flagged Assumptions section
expectations.evidence: 10 scenarios tagged @assumption with '# Assumption:' comments; Section 8 consolidates them as a table A1–A12 with the open decision and what to confirm.

expectations.text: The .md spec includes the narrative sections Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions, and Scope
expectations.evidence: Sections 1–5 are exactly Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions & Assumptions, Scope.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios cover an expired/reused reset link, an unregistered email, rate-limited reset requests, and a weak or mismatched new password
expectations.evidence: 7.13 expired link, 7.2 reload-after-completed reuse, 7.9 unregistered email, 7.3 per-address rate limit, 7.1 weak-password Scenario Outline + mismatched confirmation — all present.
